game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters

element_introduction_order.element: Four playable Turtles (Leonardo, Raphael, Michelangelo, Donatello)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects one Turtle as their main character; story establishes intra-team tournament premise
Standard fighting game conventions; weak and strong variants of each
Each character has unique move set; discovered through combat

element_introduction_order.element: Splinter power-up (flying monitor drops red ball)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Random spawn during battles; grants temporary special ability when retrieved
Requires filling green bar through successful hits; activated by pressing both strong buttons simultaneously. Japanese version adds more voice clarity
Familiar TMNT character, serves as skill gate before final boss
Unique large character model with sprite flicker on NES; based on action figure and Archie Comics lore
Serves as tournament objective and narrative climax; only boss encounter

element_introduction_order.element: Secondary roster (War, Aska, Wingnut, Chrome Dome, Armaggon)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Tournament/Versus modes; Story Mode encounters as opponents
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available in competitive modes; encountered progressively in story as rival fighters or story-relevant characters
Colored variants of playable Turtles; tests player mastery of chosen character against same moveset

typical_relief_triggers: Story Mode travel between locations (Turtle Blimp scenes)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-round match structure with escalating opponent difficulty; story mode paces progression through geographical travel (US map via Turtle Blimp) between fights, creating narrative rhythm alongside combat intensity

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction via Story Mode with three Turtle vs. Turtle matches (establishes baseline and mechanic familiarity)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progression through secondary roster and special move mastery; Splinter power-up resource management becomes tactical; Japanese version rebalances move damage and reach for tighter difficulty tuning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Hothead encounter (large character with sprite flickering on NES—technical difficulty spike); Shredder/Cyber Shredder as final skill check requiring full mastery of special attacks and positioning
notable_spikes: Cyber Shredder boss fight (final tournament match)
Japanese version: Studio 6 stage with breakable walls adds environmental interaction complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear tournament ladder with narrative wrapper (rescuing April O'Neil and Splinter from Karai). Player travels US via Turtle Blimp between fights, collecting information. Four Turtles playable; all other characters are opponents or boss encounters. Three-round match format (first to two rounds wins).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Story Mode: Leo/Raphael/Mikey/Donnie vs. sequential opponents → Casey Jones → Hothead → Shredder

notable_level_patterns.description: Best-of-three rounds per match. Each round has independent life bars. Mutagen Meter (green bar) fills through successful hits; super special attack requires both strong buttons and full meter. Power-up spawn (Splinter monitor with red ball) appears randomly mid-fight for either player to claim.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any Story Mode fight or Versus mode match

notable_level_patterns.description: Tournament mode (4-player, NES) and Versus modes allow any character (including clones of playable Turtles). SNES version restricts story playables to the four Turtles but offers secondary roster (War, Aska, Wingnut, Chrome Dome, Armaggon) as opponents or competitive entries.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tournament Mode (NES); Versus CPU mode (all versions)

notable_level_patterns.description: Japanese release (Dec 1993) rebalances character move damage and reach. Studio 6 stage gains breakable walls (feature parity with Genesis version). Voice acting replaces announcer and Turtle voices; includes new voice clips from Turtles in Time for move clarity.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages, particularly Studio 6; any character move usage in JP version vs. US version

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - TMNT Tournament Fighters SNES article)
wikipedia (Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters article)
